metadata.title:
RESERVOIR
DAMAGES


PROPERTY

so SAYS REAL ESTATE MAN,
CALLED AS AN EXPERT IN
CITY CASE FOR POSSESSION
OF GROUND.

THIRD TO HALF LOSS

pages.raw_text:
w. G. MalIoy, local real estate
man, who is a witness In the con-
demnation suit Of the city of Spo-
kane against the kiernan-Lawyer
Land Co., testified this morning be-
fore Judge Huneke that the install
Iation of the proposed reservoir
will damage adjoining Lincoln
Heights property from 33 1-3 to so
per cent of it's present value. NAL
10y was the first expert to offer
testimony for the defendants.


Malloy also testified that the
Lincoln Heights lots in that vicin-
ity were worth from $500 to $3.50,
while the city's experts placed the
maximum value at $500.


The defendants expect to intro
duce their remaining testimony
this afternoon, which will probably
mean the giving of the case to the
jury Friday night or Saturday
morning.


The city seeks to acquire 1876
acres to be used for a new reser-
voir. :.
